attributes.description: Alerts when `iis.network.blocked` increases — IIS is enforcing bandwidth throttling and blocking traffic. Indicates configured bandwidth limits are being hit.
attributes.name: [IIS OTel] Bandwidth throttling detected

investigation_guide.blob:
## IIS bandwidth throttling detected

### What fired
`INCREASE(iis.network.blocked) > 0` on at least one host during the window.

### Why it matters
IIS supports per-site bandwidth limits. When traffic exceeds the configured limit, IIS blocks additional bytes and the counter increments. Sustained blocking causes user-visible latency or transfer failures.

### Triage
1. Identify the affected host and (via the Sites & Pools dashboard) the busiest sites.
2. Compare against the configured `bandwidthLimit` for each site.
3. Look for traffic spikes from specific clients.

### Remediation
- Raise the per-site bandwidth limit if the workload has legitimately grown.
- Throttle abusive clients at the edge / WAF.

### Tuning
- Fires on any non-zero increase. If brief spikes are tolerable, raise the threshold or add an `alertDelay`.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-iisreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE iis.network.blocked IS NOT NULL
| STATS blocked_increase = SUM(INCREASE(iis.network.blocked)) BY host.name
| WHERE blocked_increase > 0
| SORT blocked_increase DESC
| LIMIT 20
